# Support and Resources

## IT Service Center (ITSZ)

The IT Service Center is the central point of contact for all IT-related questions and issues at TUM.

### Contact Information

::: {.callout-tip}
## IT Service Center Contact
| Channel | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Email** | it-support@tum.de |
| **Phone** | +49.89.289.17123 |
| **Website** | [www.it.tum.de](https://www.it.tum.de) |
| **Address** | Arcisstr. 21, 80333 München |
:::

### Support Request Process

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Support Request Workflow"
flowchart TB
    Issue["Issue Identified"]
    Issue --> Self["Self-Service Check"]
    Self --> |Resolved| Done["Issue Resolved"]
    Self --> |Not Resolved| Contact["Contact IT Service Center"]

    Contact --> Email["Email Request"]
    Contact --> Phone["Phone Call"]

    Email --> Ticket["Ticket Created"]
    Phone --> Ticket

    Ticket --> Triage["Issue Triage"]
    Triage --> Resolution["Issue Resolution"]
    Resolution --> Done
```

### What to Include in Support Requests

| Information | Description | Example |
|-------------|-------------|---------|
| **Subject** | Clear, specific title | "Server not reachable - dept-web-01" |
| **Description** | Detailed issue description | What happened, when, what you tried |
| **System** | Affected system/service | Server name, service name |
| **Error Messages** | Exact error text | Copy/paste or screenshot |
| **Steps to Reproduce** | How to recreate the issue | Step-by-step instructions |
| **Impact** | Who/what is affected | "10 researchers cannot access files" |
| **Contact** | Your contact information | Phone for urgent follow-up |

### Priority Guidelines

| Priority | Criteria | Response |
|----------|----------|----------|
| **Critical** | Service completely down, affecting many users | Same day |
| **High** | Major functionality impacted | Next business day |
| **Medium** | Partial functionality, workaround available | Within week |
| **Low** | Minor issue, enhancement request | Scheduled |

## Consulting Services

### Overview

The IT Service Center offers consulting services for administrators, particularly valuable for:

- New administrators getting started
- Planning infrastructure changes
- System integration projects
- Evaluating new requirements

### Consultation Topics

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Consulting Services Available"
mindmap
    root((IT Consulting))
        Onboarding
            System Introduction
            Tool Training
            Service Overview
        Infrastructure
            Server Planning
            Storage Design
            Network Architecture
        Integration
            LDAP Integration
            Service Connections
            Custom Solutions
        Planning
            Capacity Planning
            Migration Projects
            Security Review
```

### Requesting a Consultation

1. **Email**: it-support@tum.de
2. **Subject**: Consultation Request - [Topic]
3. **Include**:
   - Your role and department
   - Topic for consultation
   - Preferred timeframe
   - Any specific questions
4. **Schedule**: IT Service Center will arrange a meeting time

## Self-Service Resources

### TUMonline

The primary self-service tool for administrators:

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **User Management** | Manage user accounts and groups |
| **Function Objects** | Create shared mailboxes, calendars |
| **Group Management** | Create and manage groups |
| **Mailing Lists** | Set up distribution lists |

**Access**: [campus.tum.de](https://campus.tum.de)

### TUM Wiki

TUM maintains comprehensive documentation:

- Detailed service documentation
- Step-by-step guides
- Best practices
- FAQs and troubleshooting

### LRZ Documentation

LRZ provides technical documentation at [doku.lrz.de](https://doku.lrz.de):

- Server and cloud service guides
- Network documentation
- Client management resources
- Storage and backup documentation

## Training and Orientation

### New Administrator Orientation

The IT Service Center provides orientation for new administrators covering:

1. **TUM IT Overview**: Services and organization
2. **TUMonline Training**: Hands-on tool training
3. **Service Access**: How to use various services
4. **Best Practices**: Recommendations and guidelines
5. **Q&A**: Address specific questions

### Scheduling Orientation

Contact it-support@tum.de to schedule:

- Individual orientation session
- Group training for multiple new admins
- Refresher training on specific topics

## Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ)

For high-performance computing and advanced infrastructure needs:

| Service | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **HPC Clusters** | High-performance computing |
| **Large Storage** | Research data storage |
| **Scientific Computing** | Specialized computing resources |
| **Network** | Munich Scientific Network (MWN) |
| **Cloud Services** | LRZ Compute Cloud |
| **Server Hosting** | Managed virtual/physical servers |

## TUM IT Governance Structure

Understanding the TUM IT organization:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "TUM IT Governance"
flowchart TB
    CIO["CIO"]
    CIO --> IMB["Information Management Board"]
    CIO --> DXO["Digital Transformation Office"]
    CIO --> ITSZ["IT Service Center (ITSZ)"]

    ITSZ --> Schools["School IT Operations"]
    Schools --> Admins["Department IT Administrators"]

    LRZ["LRZ"] --> |provides infrastructure| ITSZ
```

| Body | Role |
|------|------|
| **CIO** | Chief Information Officer |
| **Information Management Board** | Strategic IT decisions |
| **Digital Transformation Office (DXO)** | Digital transformation initiatives |
| **IT Service Center (ITSZ)** | Central IT operations |

## IT Security at TUM

TUM provides comprehensive IT security resources:

| Resource | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **Vulnerability Management** | Security scanning and remediation |
| **Security Awareness** | Training and campaigns |
| **Security Guidelines** | Policies and best practices |
| **Incident Response** | Security incident handling |

Security resources for administrators:

- Secure workplace configuration
- Protection against malware
- Password security guidelines
- Mobile device security
- Email security

## Emergency Contacts

### Security Incidents

For security-related emergencies:

1. **Immediately contact**: IT Service Center (it-support@tum.de)
2. **Document**: What happened and when
3. **Isolate**: If possible, disconnect affected systems
4. **Preserve**: Don't delete logs or evidence

### Service Outages

For critical service outages:

- Email it-support@tum.de with URGENT in subject
- Call +49.89.289.17123
- Check [www.it.tum.de](https://www.it.tum.de) for status updates

## Feedback and Suggestions

### Providing Feedback

The IT Service Center welcomes feedback on:

- Service quality
- Documentation improvements
- New service requests
- Process improvements

**How to submit**: Email it-support@tum.de with subject "Feedback"

## Quick Reference Card

### Essential Contacts

| Purpose | Contact |
|---------|---------|
| **General Support** | it-support@tum.de |
| **Phone** | +49.89.289.17123 |
| **Website** | [www.it.tum.de](https://www.it.tum.de) |

### Essential Links

| Resource | URL |
|----------|-----|
| **TUM IT for Admins** | [www.it.tum.de/it/admins/](https://www.it.tum.de/it/admins/) |
| **TUMonline** | [campus.tum.de](https://campus.tum.de) |
| **LRZ Documentation** | [doku.lrz.de](https://doku.lrz.de) |
